     sessionId,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5574:Description: `insertEdge()` synthesizes missing nodes without any `sessionId`, but every session-aware reader/filter only trusts `node.sessionId` or `node.metadata.sessionId`. That means a caller can build a graph entirely through the public insert API, attach `metadata.sessionId` to edges, and still lose those nodes from `getNodes()` and any signal helper that depends on node-level session matching. This makes the session-aware export surface easy to misuse and hard to reason about.  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5581:sessionId: typeof metadata.sessionId === 'string' && metadata.sessionId ? metadata.sessionId : undefined,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5584:if (typeof node.sessionId === 'string' && node.sessionId) return node.sessionId;\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5586:if (matchesSession(graph, node, sessionId, 'node')) results.push(node);\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5588:Recommendation: Centralize graph/session normalization in one shared helper and either propagate `metadata.sessionId` into synthesized nodes or add an explicit node upsert API. Add direct session-scoped tests for `getNodes()`, `getEdges()`, `getEdgesFrom()`, `getEdgesTo()`, and the signal helpers.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5596:Description: The CJS helper exports `computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId)` as a recent-edge counter, while the canonical runtime implementation exports `computeMomentum(specFolder, loopType)` as a snapshot-to-snapshot delta calculator in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:555). Same name, different meaning. That is a long-term drift trap for maintainers, especially because the requested test suite only validates the CJS behavior in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts:117).  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5600:function computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5641:function reportContradictions(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5647:function contradictionDensity(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5681:Description: `insertEdge()` synthesizes missing nodes without any `sessionId`, but every session-aware reader/filter only trusts `node.sessionId` or `node.metadata.sessionId`. That means a caller can build a graph entirely through the public insert API, attach `metadata.sessionId` to edges, and still lose those nodes from `getNodes()` and any signal helper that depends on node-level session matching. This makes the session-aware export surface easy to misuse and hard to reason about.  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5688:sessionId: typeof metadata.sessionId === 'string' && metadata.sessionId ? metadata.sessionId : undefined,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5691:if (typeof node.sessionId === 'string' && node.sessionId) return node.sessionId;\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5693:if (matchesSession(graph, node, sessionId, 'node')) results.push(node);\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5695:Recommendation: Centralize graph/session normalization in one shared helper and either propagate `metadata.sessionId` into synthesized nodes or add an explicit node upsert API. Add direct session-scoped tests for `getNodes()`, `getEdges()`, `getEdgesFrom()`, `getEdgesTo()`, and the signal helpers.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5703:Description: The CJS helper exports `computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId)` as a recent-edge counter, while the canonical runtime implementation exports `computeMomentum(specFolder, loopType)` as a snapshot-to-snapshot delta calculator in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:555). Same name, different meaning. That is a long-term drift trap for maintainers, especially because the requested test suite only validates the CJS behavior in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts:117).  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5707:function computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5748:function reportContradictions(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5754:function contradictionDensity(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:3:description: \\\"Four-phase implementation delivering runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across 100 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research and sk-deep-review.\\\"\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:60:Phases were delivered in strict dependency order: runtime truth first, then coverage graph, then wave execution, then offline optimization. The original four phases were independently validated against their task lists and quality gates.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:71:1. **Lane 1 — Claim-Adjudication Stop Gate Wiring** closed F002 and F007 by adding a universal STOP veto pre-check and a dedicated `claimAdjudicationGate` in the review legal-stop decision tree, plus persisted `claim_adjudication` events, typed packet docs in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md` §9, and matching `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md` §Step 4a. New REQs: REQ-026, REQ-027.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:72:2. **Lane 2 — Coverage-Graph Session Isolation** closed F004, F005, F006, and F013 by bumping `deep-loop-graph.sqlite` to v2 with a composite primary key of `(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)` on both `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges`, migrating via drop-and-recreate, adding a shared-ID collisions regression to `session-isolation.vitest.ts`, and documenting the typed `graphEvents` payload shape and namespace rules in both state_format references. New REQs: REQ-028, REQ-029.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:75:5. **Lane 5 — Release-Readiness Reconciliation** closed F013, F014, F015, and F016 by rewriting the phase-008 closeout surfaces, the packet-root completion surfaces below, and the reducer-owned `ACTIVE RISKS` dashboard section so non-P0 release-readiness debt stays visible. New REQ: REQ-034.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:114:| Closing-audit Lane 1–5 remediation (REQ-026 through REQ-034) | PASS — see `008-further-deep-loop-improvements/implementation-summary.md` §Closing Audit Remediation Notes and `review/review-report.md` |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:42:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D3: Is the mutation coverage graph namespace (`loop_type: \\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\"`) properly isolated from the deep-research/deep-review namespaces in the shared SQLite store?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:72:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D5: Does any loop phase (init / iteration / convergence / synthesis) actively READ from the coverage graph (`coverage-graph-query.ts`, `coverage-graph-convergence.cjs`) to inform decisions, or only WRITE to it?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:90:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D5: Does the coverage graph's contribution to the 3-signal convergence math exceed its weight, or is it merely nominal?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:301:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"`sk-improve-agent` follows a different path entirely: its resume contract replays a local \\\\\\\"coverage graph + registry\\\\\\\", and `mutation-coverage.cjs` persists an `improvement` JSON graph with `fs`/`path` helpers and `loopType: 'improvement'`, while the agent only consumes an orchestrator-provided summary as advisory context. That means it does not supply the missing shared `deep_loop_graph_*` bridge for research/review convergence (.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:9-10; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:21; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:50-67; .opencode/agent/agent-improver.md:175-177).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:319:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"Both deep-loop convergence contracts still document a reducer-owned in-memory graph bridge that is not present in the live reducer: deep research says \\\\\\\"the reducer builds an in-memory coverage graph\\\\\\\" when `graphEvents` exist, and deep review says the same for review iterations (.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:1181-1205; .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:658-683).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:711:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published deep-research contract says that when `graphEvents` exist, \\\\\\\"the reducer builds an in-memory coverage graph and derives additional signals for the legal-stop gate evaluation,\\\\\\\" including graph coverage sub-checks (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:1181-1205`). The live research reducer does not implement that bridge: its only imports are `fs` and `path`, and its convergence score is taken from `latestIteration.convergenceSignals?.compositeStop` or `latestIteration?.newInfoRatio` rather than any graph helper (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:11-13`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:244-249`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1177:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The resume contract promises richer recovery than the shipped journal helpers provide. The skill says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the journal module only exposes raw-event readback, `getLastIteration()`, and `getSessionResult()`. If an interruption happens before a `session_ended` event is written, `getSessionResult()` returns `{ stopReason: null, sessionOutcome: null }`, so the helper surface itself does not reconstruct candidate, gate, benchmark, or trade-off state (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-300`, `.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:332-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:148-203`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1263:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published resume contract promises more reconstruction than the shipped recovery helper exposes. Both the command and skill say resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the only recovery-facing journal helpers return the max iteration number and the terminal `stopReason/sessionOutcome`; there is no helper that rebuilds blocked-stop payloads, trade-off state, benchmark warnings, or lineage context from raw journal events (`.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:334-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-295`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:168-203`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1530:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The shipped deep-review reducer mirrors the same pattern. `computeConvergenceScore()` reads `latest.convergenceSignals.compositeStop` or falls back to `1 - newFindingsRatio`, and the reducer then publishes only that scalar as `convergenceScore`. No inspected review reducer code reconstructs a coverage graph or invokes graph convergence logic before writing the dashboard/registry (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:360-390`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:579-583`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:671-680`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1551:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The standalone graph helper does have bounded weight, not runaway influence: `computeGraphConvergence()` builds `graphScore` from fragmentation (0.25), normalized depth (0.25), question coverage (0.30), and source diversity (0.20), then limits the merged result to `blendedScore = compositeStop * 0.6 + graphScore * 0.4`. Inside this helper, the coverage graph cannot contribute more than 40% of the final blended scalar (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-convergence.cjs:141-205`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1735:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"A different namespace-isolation weakness does exist on the shared read side for research/review sessions: the convergence handler never accepts `sessionId`, constructs its namespace from only `{ specFolder, loopType }`, and then calls `getStats()` plus `computeSignals()` / `computeMomentum()` without session scoping, so convergence decisions aggregate all sessions sharing the same spec folder and loop type (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:47-52`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:92-111`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:511-545`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:163-220`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1779:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published `sk-improve-agent` contract and visible auto workflow keep that mutation coverage graph local to the improvement runtime rather than bridging it into the shared coverage-graph MCP. The skill describes `scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs` as the reader/writer for the coverage graph and says it uses `loop_type: \\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\"` isolation, while the auto workflow only appends the state ledger and runs `scripts/reduce-state.cjs`; I found no visible `deep_loop_graph_upsert` step on this path (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:298-300`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:389-390`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:149-155`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1801:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The query surface only partially exposes session isolation. `handleCoverageGraphQuery()` accepts `sessionId` and passes it into `ns`, but the inspected helper queries for coverage gaps, contradictions, and unverified claims read by `spec_folder` and `loop_type` only, ignoring `ns.sessionId`; this means research/review sessions can be co-mingled on read even though improvement-mode entries are blocked from the DB entirely (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:28-35`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:55-58`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:63-126`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:136-147`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:238-277`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1823:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The shared SQLite coverage graph does not admit `improvement` loop types at all. The DB layer defines `LoopType = 'research' | 'review'`, constrains valid kinds/relations by those two namespaces, and the schema enforces `CHECK(loop_type IN ('research', 'review'))`; the MCP upsert handler repeats the same validation and rejects any other value (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:17`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:136-146`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-157`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:51-53`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:72-80`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2121:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"`SKILL.md` says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch and that the orchestrator skips mutation types already in the exhausted log, but the visible autonomous loop never invokes `mutation-coverage.cjs` before candidate generation. The shipped loop proceeds from integration scan directly to candidate generation, scoring, benchmarking, ledger append, reducer refresh, and reducer-driven stop check, so coverage state does not visibly steer mutation focus selection. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:137-158`)\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2513:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"I did not find a shipped orchestrator implementation file that performs the promised “replay journal + coverage graph + registry before dispatch,” so this pass had to prove absence via reducer inputs and helper interfaces rather than a single end-to-end resume driver.\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2558:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"A command-doc requirement to invoke structural graph tools before or during iterations; the inspected command surfaces only bootstrap CocoIndex.\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-strategy.md:130:### Dashboard-only ancestry surfacing: covered.\\\"\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/dispatch-20-iterations.sh:145:sessionId: $SESSION_ID\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/dispatch-20-iterations.sh:172:  echo \\\"{\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"iteration\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"mode\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"review\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"run\\\\\\\":$i,\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"complete\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"focus\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"$DIM — $(echo \\\"$FOCUS\\\" | head -c memory-platform redesign work from other research bundles unless it is directly required by the scoped runtime improvements in this packet.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7:- The standalone graph helper does have bounded weight, not runaway influence: `computeGraphConvergence()` builds `graphScore` from fragmentation (0.25), normalized depth (0.25), question coverage (0.30), and source diversity (0.20), then limits the merged result to `blendedScore = compositeStop * 0.6 + graphScore * 0.4`. Inside this helper, the coverage graph cannot contribute more than 40% of the final blended scalar (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-convergence.cjs:141-205`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:10:- The shipped deep-review reducer mirrors the same pattern. `computeConvergenceScore()` reads `latest.convergenceSignals.compositeStop` or falls back to `1 - newFindingsRatio`, and the reducer then publishes only that scalar as `convergenceScore`. No inspected review reducer code reconstructs a coverage graph or invokes graph convergence logic before writing the dashboard/registry (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:360-390`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:579-583`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:671-680`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:4:sessionId: rvw-2026-04-12T11-30-00Z\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:32:This bundle delivered runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across ~200 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research, sk-deep-review, and sk-improve-agent.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:59:Prior session context: A previous 10-iteration review (rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z) found 16 findings (0 P0 / 10 P1 / 6 P2) with CONDITIONAL verdict. Define segment-local JSONL or state artifacts and deterministic merge rules into the main packet lineage using explicit keys: `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:6220:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:147:| REQ-004 | Segment-local JSONL and merge rules MUST preserve auditability when wave results are merged back into the main packet lineage. | Segment artifacts remain replayable on their own, merged records always include explicit keys `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, and merge logic never relies on append order as the source of truth. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:6269:- Description: Phase 003 requires merge lineage to be keyed by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, but `mergeFinding()` looks up existing records with `f.findingId === findingRecord.findingId`. That means two different segment-local findings that both use `F-001` are treated as the same logical record even when their evidence differs. The same defect appears in segment-state merge logic (`wave-segment-state.cjs:199-247`), so cross-segment collisions can silently flatten lineage during board updates and replay merges.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:6320:- Description: Phase 003 requires merge lineage to be keyed by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, but `mergeFinding()` looks up existing records with `f.findingId === findingRecord.findingId`. That means two different segment-local findings that both use `F-001` are treated as the same logical record even when their evidence differs. The same defect appears in segment-state merge logic (`wave-segment-state.cjs:199-247`), so cross-segment collisions can silently flatten lineage during board updates and replay merges.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-005.md:22:- **F011**: Resume-event examples remain skeletal even where the visible state contract expects lineage metadata — `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` — The research loop protocol still shows `{\\\"type\\\":\\\"event\\\",\\\"event\\\":\\\"resumed\\\",\\\"fromIteration\\\":N}` at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:142`, while the review loop protocol only upgrades that example to `sessionId` / `generation` / `lineageMode` but still omits `timestamp` and the continuation fields at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md:519-522`. The state-format references describe lifecycle events as first-class JSONL rows keyed by lineage metadata (`sessionId`, `continuedFromRun`, `timestamp`, and restart/fork ancestry fields) at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:240-243` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:240-243`, and the quick references market these branches as live surfaces at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/quick_reference.md:87-90` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/quick_reference.md:83`. Even if F010 is fixed later, these examples would still train operators toward under-specified lineage rows.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-005.md:40:- Dashboard-only ancestry surfacing: The review reducer/dashboard still show only `sessionId`, `lineageMode`, and `generation`, but without a live restart/fork/completed-continue write path this stayed downstream of F010 rather than a distinct release-level defect.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-007.md:42:- Lifecycle transition persistence via reducer summaries: The reducer/test surfaces expose `lineageMode`, `generation`, and `sessionId`, but the inspected paths did not reveal any persisted `resumed`, `restarted`, `forked`, or `completed_continue` event handling.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:15:- A command-doc requirement to invoke structural graph tools before or during iterations; the inspected command surfaces only bootstrap CocoIndex.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:34:5. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:639:This plan ports the runtime truth infrastructure proven in 042 Phases 1-4 into the sk-improve-agent skill across four sub-phases. Sub-phase 5a delivers the core runtime contracts (stop-reason taxonomy, legal-stop gates, audit journal, hypothesis ledger). Sub-phase 5b adds improvement intelligence (mutation coverage graph, dimension trajectory, trade-off detection, exhausted-mutations log). Sub-phase 5c adds optional parallel candidate waves and candidate lineage. Sub-phase 5d adds scoring weight optimization and benchmark replay stability. When Opus receives a complex prompt with `--effort high`, it generates an internal chain-of-thought before responding. This matters for architecture trade-off analysis, root cause debugging, and multi-dimensional decision-making where surface-level pattern matching falls short. The reasoning is not just longer responses. It is a fundamentally different processing mode that considers alternatives, backtracks, and synthesizes before committing to an answer.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2410:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:30:**Context**: Phase 2 built a coverage graph in `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`.      sessionId,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5574:Description: `insertEdge()` synthesizes missing nodes without any `sessionId`, but every session-aware reader/filter only trusts `node.sessionId` or `node.metadata.sessionId`. That means a caller can build a graph entirely through the public insert API, attach `metadata.sessionId` to edges, and still lose those nodes from `getNodes()` and any signal helper that depends on node-level session matching. This makes the session-aware export surface easy to misuse and hard to reason about.  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5581:sessionId: typeof metadata.sessionId === 'string' && metadata.sessionId ? metadata.sessionId : undefined,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5584:if (typeof node.sessionId === 'string' && node.sessionId) return node.sessionId;\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5586:if (matchesSession(graph, node, sessionId, 'node')) results.push(node);\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5588:Recommendation: Centralize graph/session normalization in one shared helper and either propagate `metadata.sessionId` into synthesized nodes or add an explicit node upsert API. Add direct session-scoped tests for `getNodes()`, `getEdges()`, `getEdgesFrom()`, `getEdgesTo()`, and the signal helpers.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5596:Description: The CJS helper exports `computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId)` as a recent-edge counter, while the canonical runtime implementation exports `computeMomentum(specFolder, loopType)` as a snapshot-to-snapshot delta calculator in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:555). Same name, different meaning. That is a long-term drift trap for maintainers, especially because the requested test suite only validates the CJS behavior in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts:117).  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5600:function computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5641:function reportContradictions(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5647:function contradictionDensity(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5681:Description: `insertEdge()` synthesizes missing nodes without any `sessionId`, but every session-aware reader/filter only trusts `node.sessionId` or `node.metadata.sessionId`. That means a caller can build a graph entirely through the public insert API, attach `metadata.sessionId` to edges, and still lose those nodes from `getNodes()` and any signal helper that depends on node-level session matching. This makes the session-aware export surface easy to misuse and hard to reason about.  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5688:sessionId: typeof metadata.sessionId === 'string' && metadata.sessionId ? metadata.sessionId : undefined,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5691:if (typeof node.sessionId === 'string' && node.sessionId) return node.sessionId;\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5693:if (matchesSession(graph, node, sessionId, 'node')) results.push(node);\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5695:Recommendation: Centralize graph/session normalization in one shared helper and either propagate `metadata.sessionId` into synthesized nodes or add an explicit node upsert API. Add direct session-scoped tests for `getNodes()`, `getEdges()`, `getEdgesFrom()`, `getEdgesTo()`, and the signal helpers.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5703:Description: The CJS helper exports `computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId)` as a recent-edge counter, while the canonical runtime implementation exports `computeMomentum(specFolder, loopType)` as a snapshot-to-snapshot delta calculator in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:555). Same name, different meaning. That is a long-term drift trap for maintainers, especially because the requested test suite only validates the CJS behavior in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts:117).  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5707:function computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5748:function reportContradictions(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5754:function contradictionDensity(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:3:description: \\\"Four-phase implementation delivering runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across 100 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research and sk-deep-review.\\\"\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:60:Phases were delivered in strict dependency order: runtime truth first, then coverage graph, then wave execution, then offline optimization. The original four phases were independently validated against their task lists and quality gates.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:71:1. **Lane 1 — Claim-Adjudication Stop Gate Wiring** closed F002 and F007 by adding a universal STOP veto pre-check and a dedicated `claimAdjudicationGate` in the review legal-stop decision tree, plus persisted `claim_adjudication` events, typed packet docs in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md` §9, and matching `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md` §Step 4a. New REQs: REQ-026, REQ-027.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:72:2. **Lane 2 — Coverage-Graph Session Isolation** closed F004, F005, F006, and F013 by bumping `deep-loop-graph.sqlite` to v2 with a composite primary key of `(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)` on both `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges`, migrating via drop-and-recreate, adding a shared-ID collisions regression to `session-isolation.vitest.ts`, and documenting the typed `graphEvents` payload shape and namespace rules in both state_format references. New REQs: REQ-028, REQ-029.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:75:5. **Lane 5 — Release-Readiness Reconciliation** closed F013, F014, F015, and F016 by rewriting the phase-008 closeout surfaces, the packet-root completion surfaces below, and the reducer-owned `ACTIVE RISKS` dashboard section so non-P0 release-readiness debt stays visible. New REQ: REQ-034.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:114:| Closing-audit Lane 1–5 remediation (REQ-026 through REQ-034) | PASS — see `008-further-deep-loop-improvements/implementation-summary.md` §Closing Audit Remediation Notes and `review/review-report.md` |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:42:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D3: Is the mutation coverage graph namespace (`loop_type: \\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\"`) properly isolated from the deep-research/deep-review namespaces in the shared SQLite store?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:72:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D5: Does any loop phase (init / iteration / convergence / synthesis) actively READ from the coverage graph (`coverage-graph-query.ts`, `coverage-graph-convergence.cjs`) to inform decisions, or only WRITE to it?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:90:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D5: Does the coverage graph's contribution to the 3-signal convergence math exceed its weight, or is it merely nominal?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:301:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"`sk-improve-agent` follows a different path entirely: its resume contract replays a local \\\\\\\"coverage graph + registry\\\\\\\", and `mutation-coverage.cjs` persists an `improvement` JSON graph with `fs`/`path` helpers and `loopType: 'improvement'`, while the agent only consumes an orchestrator-provided summary as advisory context. That means it does not supply the missing shared `deep_loop_graph_*` bridge for research/review convergence (.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:9-10; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:21; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:50-67; .opencode/agent/agent-improver.md:175-177).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:319:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"Both deep-loop convergence contracts still document a reducer-owned in-memory graph bridge that is not present in the live reducer: deep research says \\\\\\\"the reducer builds an in-memory coverage graph\\\\\\\" when `graphEvents` exist, and deep review says the same for review iterations (.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:1181-1205; .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:658-683).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:711:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published deep-research contract says that when `graphEvents` exist, \\\\\\\"the reducer builds an in-memory coverage graph and derives additional signals for the legal-stop gate evaluation,\\\\\\\" including graph coverage sub-checks (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:1181-1205`). The live research reducer does not implement that bridge: its only imports are `fs` and `path`, and its convergence score is taken from `latestIteration.convergenceSignals?.compositeStop` or `latestIteration?.newInfoRatio` rather than any graph helper (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:11-13`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:244-249`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1177:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The resume contract promises richer recovery than the shipped journal helpers provide. The skill says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the journal module only exposes raw-event readback, `getLastIteration()`, and `getSessionResult()`. If an interruption happens before a `session_ended` event is written, `getSessionResult()` returns `{ stopReason: null, sessionOutcome: null }`, so the helper surface itself does not reconstruct candidate, gate, benchmark, or trade-off state (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-300`, `.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:332-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:148-203`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1263:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published resume contract promises more reconstruction than the shipped recovery helper exposes. Both the command and skill say resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the only recovery-facing journal helpers return the max iteration number and the terminal `stopReason/sessionOutcome`; there is no helper that rebuilds blocked-stop payloads, trade-off state, benchmark warnings, or lineage context from raw journal events (`.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:334-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-295`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:168-203`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1530:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The shipped deep-review reducer mirrors the same pattern. `computeConvergenceScore()` reads `latest.convergenceSignals.compositeStop` or falls back to `1 - newFindingsRatio`, and the reducer then publishes only that scalar as `convergenceScore`. No inspected review reducer code reconstructs a coverage graph or invokes graph convergence logic before writing the dashboard/registry (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:360-390`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:579-583`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:671-680`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1551:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The standalone graph helper does have bounded weight, not runaway influence: `computeGraphConvergence()` builds `graphScore` from fragmentation (0.25), normalized depth (0.25), question coverage (0.30), and source diversity (0.20), then limits the merged result to `blendedScore = compositeStop * 0.6 + graphScore * 0.4`. Inside this helper, the coverage graph cannot contribute more than 40% of the final blended scalar (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-convergence.cjs:141-205`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1735:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"A different namespace-isolation weakness does exist on the shared read side for research/review sessions: the convergence handler never accepts `sessionId`, constructs its namespace from only `{ specFolder, loopType }`, and then calls `getStats()` plus `computeSignals()` / `computeMomentum()` without session scoping, so convergence decisions aggregate all sessions sharing the same spec folder and loop type (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:47-52`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:92-111`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:511-545`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:163-220`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1779:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published `sk-improve-agent` contract and visible auto workflow keep that mutation coverage graph local to the improvement runtime rather than bridging it into the shared coverage-graph MCP. The skill describes `scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs` as the reader/writer for the coverage graph and says it uses `loop_type: \\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\"` isolation, while the auto workflow only appends the state ledger and runs `scripts/reduce-state.cjs`; I found no visible `deep_loop_graph_upsert` step on this path (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:298-300`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:389-390`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:149-155`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1801:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The query surface only partially exposes session isolation. `handleCoverageGraphQuery()` accepts `sessionId` and passes it into `ns`, but the inspected helper queries for coverage gaps, contradictions, and unverified claims read by `spec_folder` and `loop_type` only, ignoring `ns.sessionId`; this means research/review sessions can be co-mingled on read even though improvement-mode entries are blocked from the DB entirely (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:28-35`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:55-58`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:63-126`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:136-147`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:238-277`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1823:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The shared SQLite coverage graph does not admit `improvement` loop types at all. The DB layer defines `LoopType = 'research' | 'review'`, constrains valid kinds/relations by those two namespaces, and the schema enforces `CHECK(loop_type IN ('research', 'review'))`; the MCP upsert handler repeats the same validation and rejects any other value (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:17`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:136-146`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-157`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:51-53`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:72-80`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2121:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"`SKILL.md` says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch and that the orchestrator skips mutation types already in the exhausted log, but the visible autonomous loop never invokes `mutation-coverage.cjs` before candidate generation. The shipped loop proceeds from integration scan directly to candidate generation, scoring, benchmarking, ledger append, reducer refresh, and reducer-driven stop check, so coverage state does not visibly steer mutation focus selection. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:137-158`)\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2513:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"I did not find a shipped orchestrator implementation file that performs the promised “replay journal + coverage graph + registry before dispatch,” so this pass had to prove absence via reducer inputs and helper interfaces rather than a single end-to-end resume driver.\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2558:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"A command-doc requirement to invoke structural graph tools before or during iterations; the inspected command surfaces only bootstrap CocoIndex.\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-strategy.md:130:### Dashboard-only ancestry surfacing: |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7:- `SKILL.md` says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch and that the orchestrator skips mutation types already in the exhausted log, but the visible autonomous loop never invokes `mutation-coverage.cjs` before candidate generation. The shipped loop proceeds from integration scan directly to candidate generation, scoring, benchmarking, ledger append, reducer refresh, and reducer-driven stop check, so coverage state does not visibly steer mutation focus selection. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:137-158`)\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:2:  \\\"sessionId\\\": \\\"rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:52:      \\\"description\\\": \\\"`coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges` key rows by bare `id` only (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-192`), and both upsert paths decide update vs insert with `WHERE id = ?` instead of the full namespace (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`). A later session that reuses an earlier node or edge ID mutates the existing row rather than creating a session-local record, violating REQ-012’s `specFolder + loopType + sessionId` isolation contract (`.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:99-100`) and creating a cross-session overwrite/leak path underneath handlers that otherwise pass `sessionId`.\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:272:      \\\"description\\\": \\\"The research loop protocol still shows `{\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"event\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"event\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"resumed\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"fromIteration\\\\\\\":N}` at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:142`, while the review loop protocol only upgrades that example to `sessionId` / `generation` / `lineageMode` but still omits `timestamp` and the continuation fields at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md:519-522`. The state-format references describe lifecycle events as first-class JSONL rows keyed by lineage metadata (`sessionId`, `continuedFromRun`, `timestamp`, and restart/fork ancestry fields) at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:240-243` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:240-243`, and the quick references market these branches as live surfaces at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/quick_reference.md:87-90` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/quick_reference.md:83`. Even if F010 is fixed later, these examples would still train operators toward under-specified lineage |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/001-runtime-truth-foundation/spec.md:89:- Broader operator-shell compression, guided bootstrap, or memory-platform redesign work from other research bundles unless it is directly required by the scoped runtime improvements in this packet.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7:- The standalone graph helper does have bounded weight, not runaway influence: `computeGraphConvergence()` builds `graphScore` from fragmentation (0.25), normalized depth (0.25), question coverage (0.30), and source diversity (0.20), then limits the merged result to `blendedScore = compositeStop * 0.6 + graphScore * 0.4`. Inside this helper, the coverage graph cannot contribute more than 40% of the final blended scalar (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-convergence.cjs:141-205`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:10:- The shipped deep-review reducer mirrors the same pattern. `computeConvergenceScore()` reads `latest.convergenceSignals.compositeStop` or falls back to `1 - newFindingsRatio`, and the reducer then publishes only that scalar as `convergenceScore`. No inspected review reducer code reconstructs a coverage graph or invokes graph convergence logic before writing the dashboard/registry (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:360-390`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:579-583`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:671-680`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:4:sessionId: rvw-2026-04-12T11-30-00Z\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:32:This bundle delivered runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across ~200 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research, sk-deep-review, and sk-improve-agent.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:59:Prior session context: A previous 10-iteration review (rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z) found 16 findings (0 P0 / 10 P1 / 6 P2) with CONDITIONAL verdict. `findingId`.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:6220:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:147:| REQ-004 | Segment-local JSONL and merge rules MUST preserve auditability when wave results are merged back into the main packet lineage. | Segment artifacts remain replayable on their own, merged records always include explicit keys `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, and merge logic never relies on append order as the source of truth. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:6269:- Description: Phase 003 requires merge lineage to be keyed by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, but `mergeFinding()` looks up existing records with `f.findingId === findingRecord.findingId`. That means two different segment-local findings that both use `F-001` are treated as the same logical record even when their evidence differs. The same defect appears in segment-state merge logic (`wave-segment-state.cjs:199-247`), so cross-segment collisions can silently flatten lineage during board updates and replay merges.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:6320:- Description: Phase 003 requires merge lineage to be keyed by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, but `mergeFinding()` looks up existing records with `f.findingId === findingRecord.findingId`. That means two different segment-local findings that both use `F-001` are treated as the same logical record even when their evidence differs. The same defect appears in segment-state merge logic (`wave-segment-state.cjs:199-247`), so cross-segment collisions can silently flatten lineage during board updates and replay merges.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-005.md:22:- **F011**: Resume-event examples remain skeletal even where the visible state contract expects lineage metadata — `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` — The research loop protocol still shows `{\\\"type\\\":\\\"event\\\",\\\"event\\\":\\\"resumed\\\",\\\"fromIteration\\\":N}` at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:142`, while the review loop protocol only upgrades that example to `sessionId` / `generation` / `lineageMode` but still omits `timestamp` and the continuation fields at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md:519-522`. The state-format references describe lifecycle events as first-class JSONL rows keyed by lineage metadata (`sessionId`, `continuedFromRun`, `timestamp`, and restart/fork ancestry fields) at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:240-243` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:240-243`, and the quick references market these branches as live surfaces at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/quick_reference.md:87-90` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/quick_reference.md:83`. Even if F010 is fixed later, these examples would still train operators toward under-specified lineage rows.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-005.md:40:- Dashboard-only ancestry surfacing: The review reducer/dashboard still show only `sessionId`, `lineageMode`, and `generation`, but without a live restart/fork/completed-continue write path this stayed downstream of F010 rather than a distinct release-level defect.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-007.md:42:- Lifecycle transition persistence via reducer summaries: The reducer/test surfaces expose `lineageMode`, `generation`, and `sessionId`, but the inspected paths did not reveal any persisted `resumed`, `restarted`, `forked`, or `completed_continue` event handling.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:15:- A command-doc requirement to invoke structural graph tools before or during iterations; the inspected command surfaces only bootstrap CocoIndex.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:34:5. truth infrastructure proven in 042 Phases 1-4 into the sk-improve-agent skill across four sub-phases. Sub-phase 5a delivers the core runtime contracts (stop-reason taxonomy, legal-stop gates, audit journal, hypothesis ledger). Sub-phase 5b adds improvement intelligence (mutation coverage graph, dimension trajectory, trade-off detection, exhausted-mutations log). Sub-phase 5c adds optional parallel candidate waves and candidate lineage. Sub-phase 5d adds scoring weight optimization and benchmark replay stability. All new config fields are optional with defaults, preserving backward compatibility.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:650:- [ ] 042 Phase 2 coverage graph API confirmed to support `loop_type` namespace parameter\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:708:Add mutation coverage tracking, dimension trajectory, trade-off detection, and exhausted-mutations logging. When Opus receives a complex prompt with `--effort high`, it generates an internal chain-of-thought before responding. This matters for architecture trade-off analysis, root cause debugging, and multi-dimensional decision-making where surface-level pattern matching falls short. The reasoning is not just longer responses. It is a fundamentally different processing mode that considers alternatives, backtracks, and synthesizes before committing to an answer.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2410:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:30:**Context**: Phase 2 built a coverage graph in `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. Sub-phase 5b adds improvement intelligence (mutation coverage graph, dimension trajectory, trade-off detection, exhausted-mutations log). Sub-phase 5c adds optional parallel candidate waves and candidate lineage. Sub-phase 5d adds scoring weight optimization and benchmark replay stability. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:4704:6. **Convergence per segment**. Focus: whether each segment needs its own graph/coverage instance. Findings: convergence docs already say segment filtering should apply only to the current segment while hard stops still count all iterations and cross-segment analysis still reads the full JSONL ([convergence.md:23](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L23), [convergence.md:305](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L305)); the workers already expect the orchestrator to add `segment` and `convergenceSignals` to iteration records ([deep-research.md:189](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agent/deep-research.md#L189), [deep-review.md:253](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agent/deep-review.md#L253)); review STOP is explicitly global because it depends on total P0/P1 counts, full-dimension coverage, and global quality gates ([spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:340](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml#L340)). Novel insight: one global coverage graph plus segment-scoped queries is cleaner than spinning up a separate graph per segment. **F011**: Resume-event examples remain skeletal even where the visible state contract expects lineage metadata — `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` — The research loop protocol still shows `{\\\"type\\\":\\\"event\\\",\\\"event\\\":\\\"resumed\\\",\\\"fromIteration\\\":N}` at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:142`, while the review loop protocol only upgrades that example to `sessionId` / `generation` / `lineageMode` but still omits `timestamp` and the continuation fields at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md:519-522`. The state-format references describe lifecycle events as first-class JSONL rows keyed by lineage metadata (`sessionId`, `continuedFromRun`, `timestamp`, and restart/fork ancestry fields) at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:240-243` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:240-243`, and the quick references market these branches as live surfaces at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/quick_reference.md:87-90` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/quick_reference.md:83`. Even if F010 is fixed later, these examples would still train operators toward under-specified lineage rows.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2881:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-007.md:42:- /bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/trade-off-detector.cjs | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Improvement Journal — Append-Only Audit Journal for Improvement Sessions║
     3	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
     4	'use strict';
     5	
     6	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     7	// 1. IMPORTS
     8	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     9	const fs = require('node:fs');
    10	const path = require('node:path');
    11	
    12	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    13	// 2. CONSTANTS — Stop-Reason Taxonomy & Session Outcomes
    14	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15	
    16	/**
    17	 * Stop reasons explain WHY a session terminated.
    18	 * Research finding (P0): do not overload stopReason with outcome semantics.
    19	 * @type {Readonly<Record<string, string>>}
    20	 */
    21	const STOP_REASONS = Object.freeze({
    22	  converged: 'converged',
    23	  benchmarkPlateau: 'benchmarkPlateau',
    24	  plateau: 'plateau',
    25	  maxIterationsReached: 'maxIterationsReached',
    26	  blockedStop: 'blockedStop',
    27	  manualStop: 'manualStop',
    28	  error: 'error',
    29	  stuckRecovery: 'stuckRecovery',
    30	});
    31	
    32	/**
    33	 * Session outcomes explain WHAT happened to the candidate.
    34	 * Separate from stopReason per research finding (P0).
    35	 * @type {Readonly<Record<string, string>>}
    36	 */
    37	const SESSION_OUTCOMES = Object.freeze({
    38	  keptBaseline: 'keptBaseline',
    39	  promoted: 'promoted',
    40	  rolledBack: 'rolledBack',
    41	  advisoryOnly: 'advisoryOnly',
    42	});
    43	
    44	/**
    45	 * Valid event types for the improvement journal.
    46	 * Journal captures lifecycle events and stop decisions (ADR-001: orchestrator only).
    47	 * @type {Readonly<string[]>}
    48	 */
    49	const VALID_EVENT_TYPES = Object.freeze([
    50	  'session_start',
    51	  'session_initialized',
    52	  'integration_scanned',
    53	  'candidate_generated',
    54	  'candidate_scored',
    55	  'benchmark_completed',
    56	  'gate_evaluation',
    57	  'legal_stop_evaluated',
    58	  'blocked_stop',
    59	  'promotion_attempt',
    60	  'promotion_attempted',
    61	  'promotion_result',
    62	  'rollback',
    63	  'rollback_result',
    64	  'trade_off_detected',
    65	  'mutation_proposed',
    66	  'mutation_outcome',
    67	  'session_ended',
    68	  'session_end',
    69	]);
    70	
    71	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    72	// 3. HELPERS
    73	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    74	
    75	/**
    76	 * Validate that an event object has the required fields and a valid event type.
    77	 * @param {object} event - Event to validate
    78	 * @returns {{ valid: boolean, errors: string[] }}
    79	 */
    80	function validateEvent(event) {
    81	  const errors = [];
    82	
    83	  if (!event || typeof event !== 'object') {
    84	    return { valid: false, errors: ['Event must be a non-null object'] };
    85	  }
    86	
    87	  if (!event.eventType || typeof event.eventType !== 'string') {
    88	    errors.push('Event must have a string eventType');
    89	  } else if (!VALID_EVENT_TYPES.includes(event.eventType)) {
    90	    errors.push(`Invalid eventType: "${event.eventType}". Valid types: ${VALID_EVENT_TYPES.join(', ')}`);
    91	  }
    92	
    93	  if (event.eventType === 'session_ended' || event.eventType === 'session_end') {
    94	    if (!event.details || !event.details.stopReason) {
    95	      errors.push('session_ended/session_end events MUST include details.stopReason');
    96	    } else if (!Object.values(STOP_REASONS).includes(event.details.stopReason)) {
    97	      errors.push(`Invalid stopReason: "${event.details.stopReason}". Valid reasons: ${Object.values(STOP_REASONS).join(', ')}`);
    98	    }
    99	    if (!event.details || !event.details.sessionOutcome) {
   100	      errors.push('session_ended/session_end events MUST include details.sessionOutcome');
   101	    } else if (!Object.values(SESSION_OUTCOMES).includes(event.details.sessionOutcome)) {
   102	      errors.push(`Invalid sessionOutcome: "${event.details.sessionOutcome}". Valid outcomes: ${Object.values(SESSION_OUTCOMES).join(', ')}`);
   103	    }
   104	  }
   105	
   106	  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors };
   107	}
   108	
   109	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   110	// 4. CORE API
   111	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   112	
   113	/**
   114	 * Append an event to the improvement journal (append-only JSONL).
   115	 * ADR-001: Journal emission is orchestrator-only; this function is the single write point.
   116	 *
   117	 * @param {string} journalPath - Path to the improvement-journal.jsonl file
   118	 * @param {object} event - Event object with { eventType, iteration?, candidateId?, details? }
   119	 * @returns {{ success: boolean, errors?: string[] }}
   120	 */
   121	function emitEvent(journalPath, event) {
   122	  const enrichedEvent = {
   123	    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   124	    ...event,
   125	  };
   126	
   127	  const validation = validateEvent(enrichedEvent);
   128	  if (!validation.valid) {
   129	    return { success: false, errors: validation.errors };
   130	  }
   131	
   132	  // Ensure directory exists
   133	  const dir = path.dirname(journalPath);
   134	  fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
   135	
   136	  // Append-only: use 'a' flag (NFR-R01: survives process restart, no in-memory buffering)
   137	  const line = JSON.stringify(enrichedEvent) + '\n';
   138	  fs.appendFileSync(journalPath, line, 'utf8');
   139	
   140	  return { success: true };
   141	}
   142	
   143	/**
   144	 * Read all events from a journal file.
   145	 * Used for resume/replay semantics (REQ-AI-003).
   146	 *
   147	 * @param {string} journalPath - Path to the improvement-journal.jsonl file
   148	 * @returns {object[]} Array of parsed event objects
   149	 */
   150	function readJournal(journalPath) {
   151	  try {
   152	    const content = fs.readFileSync(journalPath, 'utf8');
   153	    return content
   154	      .split('\n')
   155	      .map((line) => line.trim())
   156	      .filter(Boolean)
   157	      .flatMap((line) => {
   158	        try {
   159	          return [JSON.parse(line)];
   160	        } catch (_err) {
   161	          return [];
   162	        }
   163	      });
   164	  } catch (_err) {
   165	    return [];
   166	  }
   167	}
   168	
   169	/**
   170	 * Get the last completed iteration number from a journal.
   171	 * Used for resume semantics — continuedFromIteration field.
   172	 *
   173	 * @param {string} journalPath - Path to the improvement-journal.jsonl file
   174	 * @returns {number} Last completed iteration number, or 0 if none
   175	 */
   176	function getLastIteration(journalPath) {
   177	  const events = readJournal(journalPath);
   178	  let maxIteration = 0;
   179	  for (const event of events) {
   180	    if (typeof event.iteration === 'number' && event.iteration > maxIteration) {
   181	      maxIteration = event.iteration;
   182	    }
   183	  }
   184	  return maxIteration;
   185	}
   186	
   187	/**
   188	 * Get the stop reason from the last session_ended event.
   189	 *
   190	 * @param {string} journalPath - Path to the improvement-journal.jsonl file
   191	 * @returns {{ stopReason: string|null, sessionOutcome: string|null }}
   192	 */
   193	function getSessionResult(journalPath) {
   194	  const events = readJournal(journalPath);
   195	  const endEvents = events.filter(
   196	    (e) => e.eventType === 'session_ended' || e.eventType === 'session_end'
   197	  );
   198	  if (endEvents.length === 0) {
   199	    return { stopReason: null, sessionOutcome: null };
   200	  }
   201	  const last = endEvents[endEvents.length - 1];
   202	  return {
   203	    stopReason: (last.details && last.details.stopReason) || null,
   204	    sessionOutcome: (last.details && last.details.sessionOutcome) || null,
   205	  };
   206	}
   207

   227	if (require.main === module) {

   230	  function findArg(name) {

   232	    for (const arg of args) {
   233	      if (arg.startsWith(prefix)) {
   234	        return arg.slice(prefix.length);
   235	      }
   236	    }
   237	    const idx = args.indexOf(`--${name}`);

   240	    }
