     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Trade-Off Detector — Cross-Dimension Regression Detection               ║
     3	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
     4	'use strict';
     5	
     6	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     7	// 1. IMPORTS
     8	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     9	const fs = require('node:fs');
    10	const path = require('node:path');
    11	
    12	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    13	// 2. CONSTANTS
    14	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15	
    16	/**
    17	 * Hard dimensions: structural integrity matters more, lower regression tolerance.
    18	 * Research finding: use < -3 threshold for hard dimensions (P1).
    19	 * @type {Readonly<string[]>}
    20	 */
    21	const HARD_DIMENSIONS = Object.freeze([
    22	  'structural',
    23	  'integration',
    24	  'systemFitness',
    25	]);
    26	
    27	/**
    28	 * Soft dimensions: more tolerance for regressions.
    29	 * Research finding: use < -5 threshold for soft dimensions (P1).
    30	 * @type {Readonly<string[]>}
    31	 */
    32	const SOFT_DIMENSIONS = Object.freeze([
    33	  'ruleCoherence',
    34	  'outputQuality',
    35	]);
    36	
    37	/**
    38	 * Default threshold for improvement detection.
    39	 * @type {number}
    40	 */
    41	const DEFAULT_IMPROVEMENT_THRESHOLD = 3;
    42	
    43	/**
    44	 * Default minimum number of trajectory points required before trade-off analysis.
    45	 * @type {number}
    46	 */
    47	const MIN_DATA_POINTS_DEFAULT = 3;
    48	
    49	/**
    50	 * Default regression thresholds.
    51	 * Research finding: +3/-3 for hard dims, +3/-5 for soft dims.
    52	 * @type {{ hard: number, soft: number }}
    53	 */
    54	const DEFAULT_REGRESSION_THRESHOLDS = Object.freeze({
    55	  hard: -3,
    56	  soft: -5,
    57	});
    58	
    59	function resolveMinDataPoints(options) {
    60	  if (Number.isInteger(options?.minDataPoints) && options.minDataPoints > 0) {
    61	    return options.minDataPoints;
    62	  }
    63	
    64	  const envValue = Number.parseInt(
    65	    process.env.SK_IMPROVE_AGENT_TRADE_OFF_MIN_DATA_POINTS || '',
    66	    10
    67	  );
    68	  if (Number.isInteger(envValue) && envValue > 0) {
    69	    return envValue;
    70	  }
    71	
    72	  return MIN_DATA_POINTS_DEFAULT;
    73	}
    74	
    75	function isPlainObject(value) {
    76	  return Boolean(value) && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
    77	}
    78	
    79	function isFiniteNumber(value) {
    80	  return typeof value === 'number' && Number.isFinite(value);
    81	}
    82	
    83	function readOptionalJson(filePath) {
    84	  try {
    85	    return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
    86	  } catch (_error) {
    87	    return null;
    88	  }
    89	}
    90	
    91	function normalizeDimensions(dimensions) {
    92	  if (Array.isArray(dimensions)) {
    93	    return dimensions.filter(
    94	      (dimension) =>
    95	        isPlainObject(dimension) &&
    96	        typeof dimension.name === 'string' &&
    97	        isFiniteNumber(dimension.score)
    98	    );
    99	  }
   100	
   101	  if (!isPlainObject(dimensions)) {
   102	    return [];
   103	  }
   104	
   105	  return Object.entries(dimensions)
   106	    .filter(([, score]) => isFiniteNumber(score))
   107	    .map(([name, score]) => ({ name, score }));
   108	}
   109	
   110	function resolveScoreOutputPath(scoreOutputPath, journalPath) {
   111	  if (typeof scoreOutputPath !== 'string' || !scoreOutputPath.trim()) {
   112	    return null;
   113	  }
   114	
   115	  if (path.isAbsolute(scoreOutputPath)) {
   116	    return scoreOutputPath;
   117	  }
   118	
   119	  return path.resolve(path.dirname(journalPath), scoreOutputPath);
   120	}
   121	
   122	function extractScoredDimensions(event, journalPath, scoreOutputCache) {
   123	  const details = isPlainObject(event.details) ? event.details : {};
   124	  const inlineDimensions = normalizeDimensions(
   125	    details.scoredDimensions || details.dimensions || event.scoredDimensions || event.dimensions
   126	  );
   127	  if (inlineDimensions.length > 0) {
   128	    return inlineDimensions;
   129	  }
   130	
   131	  const scoreOutputPath = resolveScoreOutputPath(
   132	    details.scoreOutputPath || event.scoreOutputPath,
   133	    journalPath
   134	  );
   135	  if (!scoreOutputPath) {
   136	    return [];
   137	  }
   138	
   139	  if (!scoreOutputCache.has(scoreOutputPath)) {
   140	    scoreOutputCache.set(scoreOutputPath, readOptionalJson(scoreOutputPath));
   141	  }
   142	  const scoreOutput = scoreOutputCache.get(scoreOutputPath);
   143	
   144	  return normalizeDimensions(scoreOutput?.dimensions || scoreOutput?.details?.dimensions);
   145	}
   146	
   147	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   148	// 3. CORE API
   149	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   150	
   151	/**
   152	 * Detect trade-offs between dimensions in trajectory data (REQ-AI-008).
   153	 * A trade-off exists when one dimension improves significantly while another regresses.
   154	 *
   155	 * @param {object[]} trajectoryData - Array of trajectory data points with { scores: { dim: number } }
   156	 * @param {object} [options] - { improvementThreshold?, minDataPoints?, regressionThresholds?: { hard?, soft? } }
   157	 * @returns {object[]|{state: string, dataPoints: number, minRequired: number, reason: string}} Array of detected trade-offs or insufficient-data state
   158	 */
   159	function detectTradeOffs(trajectoryData, options) {
   160	  const dataPoints = Array.isArray(trajectoryData) ? trajectoryData.length : 0;
   161	  const minDataPoints = resolveMinDataPoints(options);
   162	  if (dataPoints < minDataPoints) {
   163	    return {
   164	      state: 'insufficientData',
   165	      dataPoints,
   166	      minRequired: minDataPoints,
   167	      reason: `Trade-off detection requires at least ${minDataPoints} data points before analysis`,
   168	    };
   169	  }
   170	
   171	  const opts = {
   172	    improvementThreshold: DEFAULT_IMPROVEMENT_THRESHOLD,
   173	    minDataPoints,
   174	    regressionThresholds: { ...DEFAULT_REGRESSION_THRESHOLDS },
   175	    ...options,
   176	  };
   177	
   178	  if (options && options.regressionThresholds) {
   179	    opts.regressionThresholds = {
   180	      ...DEFAULT_REGRESSION_THRESHOLDS,
   181	      ...options.regressionThresholds,
   182	    };
   183	  }
   184	
   185	  const tradeOffs = [];
   186	  const allDimensions = [...HARD_DIMENSIONS, ...SOFT_DIMENSIONS];
   187	
   188	  // Compare consecutive trajectory points
   189	  for (let i = 1; i < trajectoryData.length; i++) {
   190	    const prev = trajectoryData[i - 1].scores || {};
   191	    const curr = trajectoryData[i].scores || {};
   192	
   193	    // Compute deltas
   194	    const deltas = {};
   195	    for (const dim of allDimensions) {
   196	      const prevScore = typeof prev[dim] === 'number' ? prev[dim] : 0;
   197	      const currScore = typeof curr[dim] === 'number' ? curr[dim] : 0;
   198	      deltas[dim] = currScore - prevScore;
   199	    }
   200	
   201	    // Find improving dimensions
   202	    const improving = allDimensions.filter(
   203	      (dim) => deltas[dim] > opts.improvementThreshold
   204	    );
   205	
   206	    // Find regressing dimensions
   207	    const regressing = allDimensions.filter((dim) => {
   208	      const threshold = HARD_DIMENSIONS.includes(dim)
   209	        ? opts.regressionThresholds.hard
   210	        : opts.regressionThresholds.soft;
   211	      return deltas[dim] < threshold;
   212	    });
   213	
   214	    // A trade-off exists when both improving and regressing dimensions are found
   215	    if (improving.length > 0 && regressing.length > 0) {
   216	      for (const impDim of improving) {
   217	        for (const regDim of regressing) {
   218	          tradeOffs.push({
   219	            improving: impDim,
   220	            regressing: regDim,
   221	            improvementDelta: deltas[impDim],
   222	            regressionDelta: deltas[regDim],
   223	            iteration: trajectoryData[i].iteration || i,
   224	          });
   225	        }
   226	      }
   227	    }
   228	  }
   229	
   230	  return tradeOffs;
   231	}
   232	
   233	/**
   234	 * Extract per-dimension score history from journal events.
   235	 *
   236	 * @param {string} journalPath - Path to the improvement-journal.jsonl file
   237	 * @returns {object[]} Array of trajectory-like objects: { iteration, scores: { dim: number } }

   251	        }

     1	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	# AGENT IMPROVER: EVALUATION LOOP (INTERACTIVE MODE)
     3	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	role: Agent Improvement Specialist using sk-improve-agent with operator approval
     5	purpose: Integration-aware agent evaluation with 5-dimension scoring, deterministic benchmarks, and guarded promotion
     6	action: Scan integration surfaces, generate dynamic profiles, score candidates across 5 dimensions, reduce evidence, and pause at each decision gate
     7	
     8	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     9	# OPERATING MODE
    10	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    11	operating_mode:
    12	  workflow: iterative_evaluation_loop
    13	  workflow_compliance: MANDATORY
    14	  workflow_execution: interactive
    15	  approvals: multi_gate
    16	  tracking: append_only_jsonl_ledger
    17	  validation: 5_dimension_deterministic_scoring
    18	  promotion: guarded_canonical_only
    19	  description: |
    20	    Executes the agent improvement loop with operator approval gates at key decision points.
    21	    Each iteration: scan integration -> propose candidate -> score across 5 dimensions -> benchmark -> reduce -> present gate.
    22	    The operator controls progression: approve each candidate generation, review scores before continuing,
    23	    and explicitly authorize any promotion. Stops when: operator chooses stop, max iterations reached,
    24	    all dimensions plateau (3+ identical scores), infra failures exceed threshold, or reducer signals shouldStop.
    25	
    26	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27	# EVALUATION PHILOSOPHY
    28	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    29	evaluation_philosophy:
    30	  principle: "Prove improvement before mutation"
    31	  approach: "Integration-aware 5-dimension evaluation with deterministic scoring"
    32	  mandate: "Every candidate is bounded, every score is repeatable, every decision is auditable"
    33	
    34	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    35	# USER INPUTS
    36	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	user_inputs:
    38	  target_path: "[TARGET_PATH] - Path to the agent .md file to evaluate. REQUIRED. Any file in .opencode/agent/*.md."
    39	  target_profile: "[TARGET_PROFILE] - Profile ID: handover, context-prime, or dynamic. Auto-derived from target_path if not explicit."
    40	  scoring_mode: "[SCORING_MODE] - 'dynamic' for 5-dimension any-agent scoring, 'static' for legacy keyword profiles. Default: dynamic."
    41	  spec_folder: "[SPEC_FOLDER] - Spec folder path for improvement runtime. REQUIRED."
    42	  max_iterations: "[MAX_ITERATIONS] - Maximum loop iterations before forced stop. Default: 5."
    43	  execution_mode: "[EXECUTION_MODE] - AUTONOMOUS or INTERACTIVE. Derived from :auto/:confirm suffix."
    44	
    45	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46	# FIELD HANDLING
    47	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    48	field_handling:
    49	  # Field mapping: target_path -> target_profile (inferred), agent_name (basename without .md)
    50	  derived_fields:
    51	    agent_name: "Basename of target_path without .md; use this as the real scan-integration.cjs agent identifier."
    52	  defaults:
    53	    scoring_mode_empty: "dynamic"
    54	    max_iterations_empty: "5"
    55	    target_profile_empty: "Derive from target_path: handover -> handover, context-prime -> context-prime, anything else -> dynamic"
    56	  validation:
    57	    target_path_required: true
    58	    spec_folder_required: true
    59	    target_path_must_exist: true
    60	    target_path_must_be_agent: "Must match .opencode/agent/*.md pattern"
    61	
    62	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    63	# CONTEXT LOADING
    64	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    65	context_loading:
    66	  trigger: "At workflow START, before phase_init"
    67	  mcp_integration:
    68	    tool: memory_search
    69	    note: "Call MCP tools directly - NEVER through Code Mode"
    70	    parameters: { query: "agent improvement context for {agent_name}", specFolder: "{spec_folder}", anchors: ['summary', 'decisions', 'state', 'improvement'] }
    71	  command_reference: "/memory:search"
    72	  when_to_skip: ["First-time evaluation of this agent", "User explicitly says 'skip context' or 'fresh start'", "No spec folder context exists"]
    73	  behavior:
    74	    if_context_found: "Surface prior improvement history, best-known scores, and dimensional trends before first iteration"
    75	    if_no_context: "Proceed to phase_init without delay"
    76	  checkpoint:
    77	    question: "Prior improvement context found for this agent. Load it?"
    78	    options:
    79	      - { label: "A) Load context", description: "Review prior scores and improvement history" }
    80	      - { label: "B) Skip", description: "Start fresh without prior context" }
    81	
    82	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    83	# SKILL REFERENCE
    84	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    85	skill_reference:
    86	  skill: sk-improve-agent
    87	  skill_md: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md
    88	  templates:
    89	    config: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json
    90	    strategy: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md
    91	    charter: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_charter.md
    92	    manifest: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/target_manifest.jsonc
    93	  scripts:
    94	    scanner: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs
    95	    profiler: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/generate-profile.cjs
    96	    scorer: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/score-candidate.cjs
    97	    benchmark: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/run-benchmark.cjs
    98	    journal: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs
    99	    reducer: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs
   100	
   101	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   102	# STATE PATHS
   103	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   104	state_paths:
   105	  runtime_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement"
   106	  candidates_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement/candidates"
   107	  config: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-config.json"
   108	  state_log: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   109	  strategy: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-strategy.md"
   110	  charter: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-charter.md"
   111	  manifest: "{spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc"
   112	  dashboard: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-dashboard.md"
   113	  registry: "{spec_folder}/improvement/experiment-registry.json"
   114	  integration_report: "{spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   115	  benchmark_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement/benchmark-runs/{target_profile}"
   116	
   117	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   118	# WORKFLOW
   119	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   120	workflow:
   121	  phase_init:
   122	    description: "Create runtime structure, scan integration surfaces, present init gate, record baseline"
   123	    steps:
   124	      gate_session_boundary:
   125	        type: approval_gate
   126	        description: "Confirm a fresh standalone session boundary before init writes"
   127	        present: |
   128	          improve-agent currently supports only fresh `new` sessions.
   129	          If `{spec_folder}/improvement` already contains prior state, archive or replace it instead of resuming in place.
   130	          Options:
   131	          A) Continue with a fresh session boundary
   132	          B) Cancel
   133	      step_create_directories:
   134	        description: "Create improvement runtime directories"
   135	        command: "mkdir -p {spec_folder}/improvement/candidates {spec_folder}/improvement/benchmark-runs/{target_profile}"
   136	      step_scan_integration:
   137	        description: "Map all surfaces the target agent touches across the repo"
   138	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs --agent={agent_name} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   139	      step_generate_profile:
   140	        description: "Generate dynamic scoring profile from the agent's own rules (skip if static profile)"
   141	        condition: "scoring_mode == 'dynamic'"
   142	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/generate-profile.cjs --agent={target_path} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/dynamic-profile.json"
   143	      step_copy_templates:
   144	        description: "Copy config, strategy, charter, and manifest templates into runtime root"
   145	        action: "Copy config, strategy, charter, and manifest templates into the runtime root"
   146	      gate_init:
   147	        type: approval_gate
   148	        description: "Operator reviews initialization before recording baseline"
   149	        present: |
   150	          Improvement packet initialized for `{target_path}` using profile `{target_profile}`.
   151	          Integration scan complete: {integration_report_summary}.
   152	          Proposal-first mode is active.
   153	          Options:
   154	          A) Record baseline and continue to first iteration
   155	          B) Cancel
   156	      step_record_baseline:
   157	        description: "Record baseline candidate state in append-only ledger"
   158	        action: "Append a baseline record for {target_path} into agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   159	      step_emit_journal_event:
   160	        description: "Emit session_start journal event before the first loop iteration begins"
   161	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_start --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"target\":\"{target_path}\",\"charter\":\"{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-charter.md\",\"startedAt\":\"{started_at}\"}'"
   162	
   163	  phase_loop:
   164	    description: "Iterative propose -> score -> benchmark -> reduce cycle with operator gates"
   165	    repeat: true
   166	    max_repeats: "{max_iterations}"
   167	    steps:
   168	      step_scan_integration:
   169	        description: "Refresh integration surface map before each iteration"
   170	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs --agent={agent_name} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   171	      gate_before_candidate:
   172	        type: approval_gate
   173	        description: "Operator authorizes candidate generation for this iteration"
   174	        present: |
   175	          Ready to generate candidate #{iteration} for `{target_path}`.
   176	          Options:
   177	          A) Generate candidate
   178	          B) Stop loop
   179	      step_generate_candidate:
   180	        description: "Dispatch @improve-agent to write one bounded candidate"
   181	        action: "Dispatch @improve-agent to write a new candidate into candidates/"
   182	      step_emit_journal_event_candidate_generated:
   183	        description: "Emit candidate_generated journal event after the candidate is written"
   184	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit candidate_generated --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"candidateId\":\"{candidate_id}\",\"candidatePath\":\"{candidate_path}\"}'"
   185	      step_record_candidate_lineage:
   186	        description: "Record the generated candidate in the packet-local lineage graph"
   187	        command: "node -e \"const lineage=require('./.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/candidate-lineage.cjs'); lineage.recordCandidate('{spec_folder}/improvement/candidate-lineage.json',{candidateId:'{candidate_id}',sessionId:'{session_id}',waveIndex:0,mutationType:'candidate-proposal',parentCandidateId:null,iteration:Number('{iteration}')});\""
   188	      step_score_candidate:
   189	        description: "Score candidate across 5 dimensions (dynamic) or keyword checks (static)"
   190	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/score-candidate.cjs --candidate={candidate_path} --baseline={target_path} --manifest={spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc --profile={target_profile} --target={target_path} --output={score_output_path}"
   191	      step_emit_journal_event_candidate_scored:
   192	        description: "Emit candidate_scored journal event after scoring completes"
   193	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit candidate_scored --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"candidateId\":\"{candidate_id}\",\"scoreOutputPath\":\"{score_output_path}\",\"weightedScore\":\"{weighted_score}\"}'"
   194	      step_record_mutation_coverage:
   195	        description: "Record mutation coverage and per-dimension trajectory from the scored candidate"
   196	        command: "node -e \"const fs=require('node:fs'); const coverage=require('./.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs'); const score=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('{score_output_path}','utf8')); const dimensions=Array.isArray(score.dimensions)?score.dimensions:[]; const scores=Object.fromEntries(dimensions.map((entry)=>[entry.name,entry.score])); coverage.recordMutation('{spec_folder}/improvement/mutation-coverage.json',{dimension:'composite',mutationType:'candidate-proposal',candidateId:'{candidate_id}',iteration:Number('{iteration}'),outcome:score.recommendation||'scored'}); coverage.recordTrajectory('{spec_folder}/improvement/mutation-coverage.json',{iteration:Number('{iteration}'),scores,weightedScore:Number('{weighted_score}')});\""
   197	      step_run_benchmark:
   198	        description: "Run fixture tests against packet-local outputs"
   199	        action: "Run profile fixtures against packet-local outputs under benchmark-runs/{target_profile}/"
   200	      step_measure_benchmark_stability:
   201	        description: "Measure replay stability for the current scored candidate and emit a repeatability report"
   202	        command: "node -e \"const fs=require('node:fs'); const stability=require('./.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/benchmark-stability.cjs'); const score=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('{score_output_path}','utf8')); const result=stability.measureStability([score],{minReplayCount:3,warningThreshold:0.95}); fs.writeFileSync('{spec_folder}/improvement/benchmark-runs/{target_profile}/iteration-{iteration}-repeatability.json',JSON.stringify(result,null,2)+'\\n');\""
   203	      step_detect_trade_offs:
   204	        description: "Analyze the scored trajectory for Pareto trade-offs before reduction"
   205	        command: "node -e \"const fs=require('node:fs'); const detector=require('./.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/trade-off-detector.cjs'); const trajectory=detector.getTrajectory('{spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl'); const tradeOffs=detector.detectTradeOffs(trajectory); fs.writeFileSync('{spec_folder}/improvement/trade-off-report.json',JSON.stringify({iteration:Number('{iteration}'),tradeOffs},null,2)+'\\n');\""
   206	      step_append_ledger:
   207	        description: "Record scored results in append-only JSONL ledger"
   208	        action: "Append prompt-score and benchmark records into agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   209	      step_reduce:
   210	        description: "Refresh dashboard, registry, and dimensional progress"
   211	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}/improvement"
   212	      gate_after_score:
   213	        type: approval_gate
   214	        description: "Operator reviews scores and decides next action"
   215	        present: |
   216	          Candidate scored. Review dimensional progress in dashboard.
   217	          Options:
   218	          A) Continue to next iteration
   219	          B) Promote accepted candidate (handover only, requires evidence)
   220	          C) Stop loop
   221	      step_emit_journal_event_gate_evaluation:
   222	        description: "Emit gate_evaluation journal event after the operator gate is evaluated"
   223	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit gate_evaluation --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"gateName\":\"gate_after_score\",\"gateDecision\":\"{gate_after_score}\",\"stopReason\":\"{stop_reason}\"}'"
   224	      step_promote_candidate:
   225	        description: "Promote the accepted candidate to canonical target (guarded, requires score evidence)"
   226	        condition: "gate_after_score == 'B' AND score_recommendation == 'candidate-better'"
   227	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/promote-candidate.cjs --candidate={candidate_path} --target={target_path} --score={score_output_path} --benchmark-report={benchmark_output_path} --repeatability-report={repeatability_output_path} --config={spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-config.json --manifest={spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc --archive-dir={spec_folder}/improvement/archive --approve"
   228	
   229	  phase_synthesis:
   230	    description: "After loop exits, summarize findings and recommend next action"
   231	    steps:
   232	      step_final_dashboard:
   233	        description: "Present final dashboard with per-dimension trends and stop reason"
   234	        action: "Read dashboard.md and present summary to operator"
   235	      step_recommendation:
   236	        description: "Recommend continue, promote, or stop based on reducer output"
   237	        action: "Check experiment-registry.json for bestPromptRecord and stopStatus"
   238	      step_emit_journal_event:
   239	        description: "Emit session_end journal event after synthesis completes"
   240	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_end --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"stopReason\":\"{stop_reason}\",\"sessionOutcome\":\"{session_outcome}\",\"endedAt\":\"{ended_at}\",\"totalIterations\":\"{total_iterations}\"}'"

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_improve-agent_auto.yaml | sed -n '1,320p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
   300	node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_start --journal specs/042/008/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{"sessionId":"imp-2026-04-11T12-00-00Z","target":"deep-research","charter":"...","startedAt":"2026-04-11T12:00:00Z"}'
   301	
   302	# At iteration boundaries:
   303	# candidate_generated after the candidate is written
   304	# candidate_scored after scoring completes
   305	# gate_evaluation after stop-check or operator-gate evaluation
   306	# The CLI form carries boundary metadata inside details because the helper's CLI does not expose top-level iteration/candidate fields.
   307	
   308	# At session end:
   309	# node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_end --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{"stopReason":"blockedStop","sessionOutcome":"advisoryOnly","endedAt":"2026-04-11T12:05:00Z","totalIterations":3}'
   310	```
   311	
   312	### Step 6C: Stop-Reason Reporting (Phase 005)
   313	
   314	After loop exits, classify the termination:
   315	
   316	**stopReason** (WHY):
   317	- `converged` — All legal-stop gate bundles passed
   318	- `maxIterationsReached` — Hit `max_iterations` limit
   319	- `blockedStop` — Convergence math triggered but gate bundles failed
   320	- `manualStop` — User cancelled
   321	- `error` — Script or infra failure
   322	- `stuckRecovery` — Stuck detection triggered and recovery exhausted
   323	
   324	**sessionOutcome** (WHAT):
   325	- `keptBaseline` — Baseline retained
   326	- `promoted` — Candidate promoted to canonical target
   327	- `rolledBack` — Promotion reversed
   328	- `advisoryOnly` — Assessment only, no mutation
   329	
   330	### Step 6D: Resume Semantics (Phase 005)
   331	
   332	When `--session-id=<prior-id>` is provided:
   333	
   334	1. Read the prior journal from `{spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl`
   335	2. Replay journal state to determine `continuedFromIteration`
   336	3. Resume the iteration counter from that point
   337	4. Do NOT repeat already-completed iterations
   338	
   339	Supported lineage modes: `new`. sk-improve-agent is one-session-only in the current release — every invocation starts a fresh session with generation 1. `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, and `completed-continue` were described in earlier drafts but have no runtime wiring; see `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md §Resume/Continuation Semantics (current release)` for the full retraction.
   340	
   341	### Step 7: Return Status
   342	
   343	- Completed normally: `STATUS=OK ITERATIONS={N} BEST_SCORE={score}`
   344	- User cancelled: `STATUS=CANCELLED`
   345	- Max iterations: `STATUS=OK REASON="max_iterations_reached"`
   346	- Plateau: `STATUS=OK REASON="all_dimensions_plateaued"`
   347	- Error: `STATUS=FAIL ERROR="{message}"`
   348	
   349	---
   350	
   351	## 5. EXAMPLES
   352	
   353	### Evaluate Handover Agent (Static Profile, Interactive)
   354	
   355	```
   356	/improve:agent ".opencode/agent/handover.md" :confirm --spec-folder=specs/041/008
   357	```
   358	
   359	### Evaluate Any Agent (Dynamic Profile, Autonomous)
   360	
   361	```
   362	/improve:agent ".opencode/agent/debug.md" :auto --iterations=3
   363	```
   364	
   365	### Quick Integration Health Check
   366	
   367	```
   368	/improve:agent ".opencode/agent/review.md" :auto --iterations=1
   369	```
   370	
   371	### Prompt for Target Selection
   372	
   373	```
   374	/improve:agent :confirm
   375	```
   376	Setup phase lists available agents and asks for selection.
   377	
   378	---
   379	
   380	## 6. EXAMPLE OUTPUT
   381	
   382	```
   383	Agent Improvement Loop Complete
   384	────────────────────────────────
   385	
   386	Target: .opencode/agent/handover.md
   387	Profile: handover (static)
   388	Scoring: 5-dimension dynamic
   389	Iterations: 3
   390	
   391	Dimensional Scores (final):
   392	  Structural:    100 (best: 100, trend: ->)
   393	  Rule Coherence: 95 (best: 95, trend: ->)
   394	  Integration:   100 (best: 100, trend: ->)
   395	  Output Quality:  90 (best: 95, trend: down)
   396	  System Fitness: 100 (best: 100, trend: ->)
   397	
   398	Weighted Score: 97/100
   399	Stop Reason: All dimensions plateaued after 3 iterations
   400	
   401	Artifacts:
   402	  Dashboard: specs/041/008/improvement/agent-improvement-dashboard.md
   403	  Registry:  specs/041/008/improvement/experiment-registry.json
   404	  Candidates: 3 written to specs/041/008/improvement/candidates/
   405	
   406	STATUS=OK ITERATIONS=3 BEST_SCORE=97 REASON="all_dimensions_plateaued"
   407	```
   408	
   409	---
   410	
   411	## 7. NOTES
   412	
   413	- **Skill dependency**: Requires `sk-improve-agent` at `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/`
   414	- **Promotion**: Only handover target with static profile is promotion-eligible. Dynamic profiles produce assessment only.
   415	- **Scoring**: All 5 dimensions are deterministic (regex, string matching, file existence). No LLM-as-judge.
   416	- **Stop rules**: Loop stops on dimension plateau (3+ identical scores), max iterations, or infra failure threshold.
   417	- **Runtime parity**: Agent exists across 4 runtimes (.opencode, .claude, .codex, .agents). Scanner checks all.
   418	
   419	---
   420	

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	# AGENT IMPROVER: EVALUATION LOOP (AUTONOMOUS MODE)
     3	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	role: Agent Improvement Specialist using sk-improve-agent autonomously
     5	purpose: Integration-aware agent evaluation with 5-dimension scoring, deterministic benchmarks, and guarded promotion
     6	action: Scan integration surfaces, generate dynamic profiles, score candidates across 5 dimensions, reduce evidence, and decide keep/reject/promote
     7	
     8	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     9	# OPERATING MODE
    10	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    11	operating_mode:
    12	  workflow: iterative_evaluation_loop
    13	  workflow_compliance: MANDATORY
    14	  workflow_execution: autonomous
    15	  approvals: none
    16	  tracking: append_only_jsonl_ledger
    17	  validation: 5_dimension_deterministic_scoring
    18	  promotion: guarded_canonical_only
    19	  description: |
    20	    Executes the agent improvement loop without step-by-step approval.
    21	    Each iteration: scan integration -> propose candidate -> score across 5 dimensions -> benchmark -> reduce -> decide.
    22	    Stops when: max iterations reached, all dimensions plateau (3+ identical scores),
    23	    infra failures exceed threshold, or reducer signals shouldStop.
    24	
    25	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	# EVALUATION PHILOSOPHY
    27	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28	evaluation_philosophy:
    29	  principle: "Prove improvement before mutation"
    30	  approach: "Integration-aware 5-dimension evaluation with deterministic scoring"
    31	  mandate: "Every candidate is bounded, every score is repeatable, every decision is auditable"
    32	
    33	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    34	# USER INPUTS
    35	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    36	user_inputs:
    37	  target_path: "[TARGET_PATH] - Path to the agent .md file to evaluate. REQUIRED. Any file in .opencode/agent/*.md."
    38	  target_profile: "[TARGET_PROFILE] - Profile ID: handover, context-prime, or dynamic. Auto-derived from target_path if not explicit."
    39	  scoring_mode: "[SCORING_MODE] - 'dynamic' for 5-dimension any-agent scoring, 'static' for legacy keyword profiles. Default: dynamic."
    40	  spec_folder: "[SPEC_FOLDER] - Spec folder path for improvement runtime. REQUIRED."
    41	  max_iterations: "[MAX_ITERATIONS] - Maximum loop iterations before forced stop. Default: 5."
    42	  execution_mode: "[EXECUTION_MODE] - AUTONOMOUS or INTERACTIVE. Derived from :auto/:confirm suffix."
    43	
    44	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    45	# FIELD HANDLING
    46	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    47	field_handling:
    48	  # Field mapping: target_path -> target_profile (inferred), agent_name (basename without .md)
    49	  derived_fields:
    50	    agent_name: "Basename of target_path without .md; use this as the real scan-integration.cjs agent identifier."
    51	  defaults:
    52	    scoring_mode_empty: "dynamic"
    53	    max_iterations_empty: "5"
    54	    target_profile_empty: "Derive from target_path: handover -> handover, context-prime -> context-prime, anything else -> dynamic"
    55	  validation:
    56	    target_path_required: true
    57	    spec_folder_required: true
    58	    target_path_must_exist: true
    59	    target_path_must_be_agent: "Must match .opencode/agent/*.md pattern"
    60	
    61	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    62	# CONTEXT LOADING
    63	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    64	context_loading:
    65	  trigger: "At workflow START, before phase_init"
    66	  mcp_integration:
    67	    tool: memory_search
    68	    note: "Call MCP tools directly - NEVER through Code Mode"
    69	    parameters: { query: "agent improvement context for {agent_name}", specFolder: "{spec_folder}", anchors: ['summary', 'decisions', 'state', 'improvement'] }
    70	  command_reference: "/memory:search"
    71	  when_to_skip: ["First-time evaluation of this agent", "User explicitly says 'skip context' or 'fresh start'", "No spec folder context exists"]
    72	  behavior:
    73	    if_context_found: "Surface prior improvement history, best-known scores, and dimensional trends before first iteration"
    74	    if_no_context: "Proceed to phase_init without delay"
    75	
    76	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    77	# SKILL REFERENCE
    78	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    79	skill_reference:
    80	  skill: sk-improve-agent
    81	  skill_md: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md
    82	  templates:
    83	    config: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json
    84	    strategy: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md
    85	    charter: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_charter.md
    86	    manifest: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/target_manifest.jsonc
    87	  scripts:
    88	    scanner: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs
    89	    profiler: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/generate-profile.cjs
    90	    scorer: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/score-candidate.cjs
    91	    benchmark: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/run-benchmark.cjs
    92	    journal: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs
    93	    reducer: .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs
    94	
    95	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    96	# STATE PATHS
    97	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    98	state_paths:
    99	  runtime_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement"
   100	  candidates_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement/candidates"
   101	  config: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-config.json"
   102	  state_log: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   103	  strategy: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-strategy.md"
   104	  charter: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-charter.md"
   105	  manifest: "{spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc"
   106	  dashboard: "{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-dashboard.md"
   107	  registry: "{spec_folder}/improvement/experiment-registry.json"
   108	  integration_report: "{spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   109	  benchmark_root: "{spec_folder}/improvement/benchmark-runs/{target_profile}"
   110	
   111	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   112	# WORKFLOW
   113	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   114	workflow:
   115	  phase_init:
   116	    description: "Create runtime structure, scan integration surfaces, record baseline"
   117	    steps:
   118	      step_session_boundary_gate:
   119	        description: "Gate the run to a fresh standalone session boundary before init writes"
   120	        verify:
   121	          - lineage_mode_must_equal_new: "Improve-agent currently ships only `new` sessions; resume/restart/fork/completed-continue are not runtime-wired."
   122	          - existing_runtime_must_be_archived: "If {spec_folder}/improvement already exists from a prior run, archive or replace it instead of resuming in place."
   123	          - dormant_resume_fields_disallowed: "Do not populate continuedFromIteration, resumeMode, or carried-forward parentSessionId state."
   124	        on_fail: "Halt with STATUS=FAIL and instruct the operator to archive the prior improvement packet before re-running."
   125	      step_create_directories:
   126	        description: "Create improvement runtime directories"
   127	        command: "mkdir -p {spec_folder}/improvement/candidates {spec_folder}/improvement/benchmark-runs/{target_profile}"
   128	      step_scan_integration:
   129	        description: "Map all surfaces the target agent touches across the repo"
   130	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs --agent={agent_name} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   131	      step_generate_profile:
   132	        description: "Generate dynamic scoring profile from the agent's own rules (skip if static profile)"
   133	        condition: "scoring_mode == 'dynamic'"
   134	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/generate-profile.cjs --agent={target_path} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/dynamic-profile.json"
   135	      step_copy_templates:
   136	        description: "Copy config, strategy, charter, and manifest templates into runtime root"
   137	        action: "Copy config, strategy, charter, and manifest templates into the runtime root"
   138	      step_record_baseline:
   139	        description: "Record baseline candidate state in append-only ledger"
   140	        action: "Append a baseline record for {target_path} into agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   141	      step_emit_journal_event:
   142	        description: "Emit session_start journal event before the first loop iteration begins"
   143	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_start --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"target\":\"{target_path}\",\"charter\":\"{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-charter.md\",\"startedAt\":\"{started_at}\"}'"
   144	
   145	  phase_loop:
   146	    description: "Iterative propose -> score -> benchmark -> reduce cycle"
   147	    repeat: true
   148	    max_repeats: "{max_iterations}"
   149	    steps:
   150	      step_scan_integration:
   151	        description: "Refresh integration surface map before each iteration"
   152	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs --agent={agent_name} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
   153	      step_generate_candidate:
   154	        description: "Dispatch @improve-agent to write one bounded candidate"
   155	        action: "Dispatch @improve-agent to write a new candidate into candidates/"
   156	      step_emit_journal_event_candidate_generated:
   157	        description: "Emit candidate_generated journal event after the candidate is written"
   158	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit candidate_generated --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"candidateId\":\"{candidate_id}\",\"candidatePath\":\"{candidate_path}\"}'"
   159	      step_record_candidate_lineage:
   160	        description: "Record the generated candidate in the packet-local lineage graph"
   161	        command: "node -e \"const lineage=require('./.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/candidate-lineage.cjs'); lineage.recordCandidate('{spec_folder}/improvement/candidate-lineage.json',{candidateId:'{candidate_id}',sessionId:'{session_id}',waveIndex:0,mutationType:'candidate-proposal',parentCandidateId:null,iteration:Number('{iteration}')});\""
   162	      step_score_candidate:
   163	        description: "Score candidate across 5 dimensions (dynamic) or keyword checks (static)"
   164	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/score-candidate.cjs --candidate={candidate_path} --baseline={target_path} --manifest={spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc --profile={target_profile} --target={target_path} --output={score_output_path}"
   165	      step_emit_journal_event_candidate_scored:
   166	        description: "Emit candidate_scored journal event after scoring completes"
   167	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit candidate_scored --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"candidateId\":\"{candidate_id}\",\"scoreOutputPath\":\"{score_output_path}\",\"weightedScore\":\"{weighted_score}\"}'"
   168	      step_record_mutation_coverage:
   169	        description: "Record mutation coverage and per-dimension trajectory from the scored candidate"
   170	        command: "node -e \"const fs=require('node:fs'); const coverage=require('./.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs'); const score=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('{score_output_path}','utf8')); const dimensions=Array.isArray(score.dimensions)?score.dimensions:[]; const scores=Object.fromEntries(dimensions.map((entry)=>[entry.name,entry.score])); coverage.recordMutation('{spec_folder}/improvement/mutation-coverage.json',{dimension:'composite',mutationType:'candidate-proposal',candidateId:'{candidate_id}',iteration:Number('{iteration}'),outcome:score.recommendation||'scored'}); coverage.recordTrajectory('{spec_folder}/improvement/mutation-coverage.json',{iteration:Number('{iteration}'),scores,weightedScore:Number('{weighted_score}')});\""
   171	      step_run_benchmark:
   172	        description: "Run fixture tests against packet-local outputs"
   173	        action: "Run profile fixtures against packet-local outputs under benchmark-runs/{target_profile}/"
   174	      step_measure_benchmark_stability:
   175	        description: "Measure replay stability for the current scored candidate and emit a repeatability report"
   176	        command: "node -e \"const fs=require('node:fs'); const stability=require('./.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/benchmark-stability.cjs'); const score=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('{score_output_path}','utf8')); const result=stability.measureStability([score],{minReplayCount:3,warningThreshold:0.95}); fs.writeFileSync('{spec_folder}/improvement/benchmark-runs/{target_profile}/iteration-{iteration}-repeatability.json',JSON.stringify(result,null,2)+'\\n');\""
   177	      step_detect_trade_offs:
   178	        description: "Analyze the scored trajectory for Pareto trade-offs before reduction"
   179	        command: "node -e \"const fs=require('node:fs'); const detector=require('./.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/trade-off-detector.cjs'); const trajectory=detector.getTrajectory('{spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl'); const tradeOffs=detector.detectTradeOffs(trajectory); fs.writeFileSync('{spec_folder}/improvement/trade-off-report.json',JSON.stringify({iteration:Number('{iteration}'),tradeOffs},null,2)+'\\n');\""
   180	      step_append_ledger:
   181	        description: "Record scored results in append-only JSONL ledger"
   182	        action: "Append prompt-score and benchmark records into agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
   183	      step_reduce:
   184	        description: "Refresh dashboard, registry, and dimensional progress"
   185	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}/improvement"
   186	      step_stop_check:
   187	        description: "Evaluate stop conditions: dimension plateau, infra failure, weak benchmarks, max iterations"
   188	        action: "Stop if experiment-registry.json reports stopStatus.shouldStop: true"
   189	      step_emit_journal_event_gate_evaluation:
   190	        description: "Emit gate_evaluation journal event after stop-condition evaluation"
   191	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit gate_evaluation --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"gateName\":\"stop_check\",\"gateResult\":\"{stop_status}\",\"stopReason\":\"{stop_reason}\"}'"
   192	
   193	  phase_synthesis:
   194	    description: "After loop exits, summarize findings and recommend next action"
   195	    steps:
   196	      step_final_dashboard:
   197	        description: "Present final dashboard with per-dimension trends and stop reason"
   198	        action: "Read dashboard.md and present summary to operator"
   199	      step_recommendation:
   200	        description: "Recommend continue, promote, or stop based on reducer output"
   201	        action: "Check experiment-registry.json for bestPromptRecord and stopStatus"
   202	      step_emit_journal_event:
   203	        description: "Emit session_end journal event after synthesis completes"
   204	        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_end --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"stopReason\":\"{stop_reason}\",\"sessionOutcome\":\"{session_outcome}\",\"endedAt\":\"{ended_at}\",\"totalIterations\":\"{total_iterations}\"}'"

.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:335:2. Replay journal state to determine `continuedFromIteration`
.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:339:Supported lineage modes: `new`. sk-improve-agent is one-session-only in the current release — every invocation starts a fresh session with generation 1. `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, and `completed-continue` were described in earlier drafts but have no runtime wiring; see `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md §Resume/Continuation Semantics (current release)` for the full retraction.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/target_manifest.jsonc:21:  "integrationScanScript": ".opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs",
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:192:2. Run `scripts/scan-integration.cjs` to discover all surfaces the target agent touches.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:285:Sessions support a single lineage mode today: `new`. Every invocation of the `/improve:agent` workflow starts a fresh session with a new session id and generation 1. Multi-generation lineage modes (`resume`, `restart`, `fork`, `completed-continue`) were described in earlier drafts but have no shipped runtime wiring in the improve-agent workflow, reducer, or journal consumer.

.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:440:| `scripts/scan-integration.cjs` | Full integration surface scanner |

.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:445:| `scripts/trade-off-detector.cjs` | Cross-dimension regression detector using trajectory and Pareto analysis |

.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:61:| 01-002 | Scan agent with no mirrors | `node scripts/scan-integration.cjs --agent=nonexistent` | `status: "complete"`, canonical `exists: false`, mirrors all `missing` | Script completes without error, reports missing surfaces |

    29	beforeEach(() => {

    37	
    38	describe('improvement-journal', () => {
    39	  describe('constants', () => {
    40	    it('exports frozen STOP_REASONS', () => {
    41	      expect(journal.STOP_REASONS).toBeDefined();
    42	      expect(Object.isFrozen(journal.STOP_REASONS)).toBe(true);
    43	      expect(journal.STOP_REASONS.converged).toBe('converged');
    44	      expect(journal.STOP_REASONS.plateau).toBe('plateau');
    45	      expect(journal.STOP_REASONS.maxIterationsReached).toBe('maxIterationsReached');
    46	      expect(journal.STOP_REASONS.blockedStop).toBe('blockedStop');
    47	      expect(journal.STOP_REASONS.manualStop).toBe('manualStop');

    49	      expect(journal.STOP_REASONS.stuckRecovery).toBe('stuckRecovery');
    50	    });
    51	
    52	    it('exports frozen SESSION_OUTCOMES', () => {
    53	      expect(journal.SESSION_OUTCOMES).toBeDefined();
    54	      expect(Object.isFrozen(journal.SESSION_OUTCOMES)).toBe(true);
    55	      expect(journal.SESSION_OUTCOMES.keptBaseline).toBe('keptBaseline');
    56	      expect(journal.SESSION_OUTCOMES.promoted).toBe('promoted');
    57	      expect(journal.SESSION_OUTCOMES.rolledBack).toBe('rolledBack');
    58	      expect(journal.SESSION_OUTCOMES.advisoryOnly).toBe('advisoryOnly');
    59	    });
    60	
    61	    it('exports VALID_EVENT_TYPES array', () => {
    62	      expect(Array.isArray(journal.VALID_EVENT_TYPES)).toBe(true);

    64	      expect(journal.VALID_EVENT_TYPES).toContain('candidate_generated');

    68	    });
    69	  });
    70	
    71	  describe('validateEvent', () => {
    72	    it('accepts valid event types', () => {

   121	  describe('emitEvent', () => {

   197	  describe('getSessionResult', () => {
   198	    it('returns nulls when no session_ended event', () => {
   199	      journal.emitEvent(journalPath, { eventType: 'session_start' });
   200	      const result = journal.getSessionResult(journalPath);
   201	      expect(result.stopReason).toBeNull();
   202	      expect(result.sessionOutcome).toBeNull();
   203	    });
   204	
   205	    it('returns stop reason and outcome from session_ended', () => {
   206	      journal.emitEvent(journalPath, {
   207	        eventType: 'session_ended',
   208	        details: {
   209	          stopReason: 'converged',
   210	          sessionOutcome: 'promoted',
   211	        },
   212	      });
   213	
   214	      const result = journal.getSessionResult(journalPath);
   215	      expect(result.stopReason).toBe('converged');
   216	      expect(result.sessionOutcome).toBe('promoted');
   217	    });
   218	
   219	    it('returns stop reason and outcome from session_end alias', () => {
   220	      journal.emitEvent(journalPath, {
   221	        eventType: 'session_end',
   222	        details: {
   223	          stopReason: 'benchmarkPlateau',
   224	          sessionOutcome: 'advisoryOnly',
   225	        },
   226	      });
   227	
   228	      const result = journal.getSessionResult(journalPath);
   229	      expect(result.stopReason).toBe('benchmarkPlateau');
   230	      expect(result.sessionOutcome).toBe('advisoryOnly');
   231	    });
   232	  });
   233	});

# Iteration 17: D3 Small-Sample Trade-Off and Stability Gating

## Focus
This iteration stayed on D3 and targeted the last open question around false positives and small-sample evidence. I inspected the trade-off detector, benchmark-stability helper, their unit tests, and the reducer/dashboard consumers together to determine whether low-sample runs can trigger warnings before enough evidence exists and whether the current operator surfaces distinguish real trade-offs from insufficient sample size.

## Findings
- `trade-off-detector.cjs` treats two trajectory points as sufficient evidence: `length < 2` is the only guard, and the detector immediately diffs each consecutive pair without consulting the shipped `trajectory.minDataPoints: 3` setting. That means one sharp delta between iteration 1 and 2 can already emit a trade-off even though the README and playbook describe a minimum 3-data-point requirement. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/trade-off-detector.cjs:64-67`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/trade-off-detector.cjs:85-127`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json:96-99`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/README.md:67`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/manual_testing_playbook/06--end-to-end-loop/023-trade-off-detection.md:19-24`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/manual_testing_playbook/06--end-to-end-loop/023-trade-off-detection.md:32-35`)
- `benchmark-stability.cjs` does not compensate for that low-sample risk because it enforces no minimum replay count at all. Any non-empty result array is eligible, `stddev()` returns `0` for a single sample, `stabilityCoefficient()` converts that to `1.0`, and `measureStability()` therefore marks one-sample dimensions as fully stable; the configured `benchmarkStability.replayCount: 3` is descriptive only in the inspected helper path. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/benchmark-stability.cjs:20-27`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/benchmark-stability.cjs:69-92`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/benchmark-stability.cjs:103-171`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json:120-123`)
- The shipped tests lock in that under-constrained behavior instead of guarding against it. Trade-off tests only rule out `0` or `1` trajectory point and then accept a 2-point trade-off as valid, while benchmark-stability tests explicitly accept a 2-replay sample as stable and only check instability once there are 3 replay results with visible variance. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/tests/trade-off-detector.vitest.ts:58-79`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/tests/benchmark-stability.vitest.ts:80-121`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/tests/benchmark-stability.vitest.ts:124-139`)
- Reducer stop-gating collapses every non-`benchmark-pass` benchmark record into `benchmarkFailCount` and `weakBenchmarkRuns`, then stops after the configured maximum of 2 weak runs, but it never reads replay counts, per-dimension coefficients, or `stabilityWarning` payloads. In practice the operator-facing stop reason cannot distinguish “unstable because sample size is too small” from “genuine benchmark regression.” (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:173-186`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:259-289`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json:72-79`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config_reference.md:57-60`)
- The published dashboard contract promises reducer-populated trade-off visibility, but the shipped renderer does not surface it. `improvement_strategy.md` reserves a reducer-owned “Trade-Off Detection” table, yet `reduce-state.cjs` only renders counts, repeated failure modes, dimensional progress, and a generic benchmark-failure recommendation, so neither real Pareto trade-offs nor insufficient-sample instability are clearly labeled in the visible dashboard path. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md:123-136`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:371-438`)

## Ruled Out
- This is not primarily a threshold-value bug: the shipped trade-off thresholds and weak-benchmark stop thresholds are internally consistent with config, so the problem is missing sample-size enforcement and signal loss across consumers, not a simple off-by-one constant mismatch. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json:72-79`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json:100-123`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/trade-off-detector.cjs:40-49`)
- Benchmark stability is not over-blocking small samples in the inspected helper path; it under-blocks them by allowing 1-2 replay samples to look stable enough unless visible variance already appears. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/benchmark-stability.cjs:69-92`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/benchmark-stability.cjs:103-171`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/tests/benchmark-stability.vitest.ts:124-139`)

## Dead Ends
- I did not find a visible orchestrator implementation file that wires `trade-off-detector.cjs` and `benchmark-stability.cjs` together end-to-end, so the compensation question had to be answered from the shipped helpers, tests, reducer inputs, and dashboard renderer rather than a single runtime driver.

## Sources Consulted
- `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/deep-research-strategy.md:42-43`
- `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7-11`
- `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:7-11`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/trade-off-detector.cjs:64-127`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/benchmark-stability.cjs:20-191`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:173-186`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:259-289`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:371-438`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/tests/trade-off-detector.vitest.ts:58-79`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/tests/benchmark-stability.vitest.ts:80-139`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json:72-123`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md:123-136`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/README.md:67`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/manual_testing_playbook/06--end-to-end-loop/023-trade-off-detection.md:19-35`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/manual_testing_playbook/07--runtime-truth/029-benchmark-stability.md:64-80`

## Reflection
- What worked and why: Reading the two D3 helpers beside their tests and the reducer made it possible to separate helper-level sample-size behavior from downstream operator surfacing.
- What did not work and why: I could not anchor the helper interaction to a single visible orchestrator runtime file, so some of the “does gating compensate?” answer had to come from omission across shipped consumer surfaces.
- What I would do differently: I would next pair this static audit with a packet-local fixture run so the 2-point trade-off and 1-2 replay stability edge cases can be demonstrated with concrete output rather than code inference alone.

## Recommended Next Focus
Rotate to D4 and audit self-compliance drift in `sk-improve-agent`'s published runtime contracts. The most productive next pass is to compare the README, SKILL, `assets/improvement_strategy.md`, and the visible reducer/dashboard path to verify which promised operator-visible artifacts actually survive end-to-end, especially around trade-off annotations, benchmark stability labeling, and stop-reason/session-outcome wording. That would convert this D3 evidence into a broader contract-compliance answer without reopening already-closed research/review findings.

# Iteration 18: D4 sk-improve-agent Contract Artifact Survival

## Focus
This iteration audited contract self-compliance for `sk-improve-agent` by comparing the published operator-facing promises in the README, SKILL, changelog, and `assets/improvement_strategy.md` against the visible reducer/dashboard path in `scripts/reduce-state.cjs`. The goal was to verify which promised artifacts actually survive end-to-end and where trade-off labeling, benchmark stability wording, and stop-reason/session-outcome language drift.

## Findings
- `assets/improvement_strategy.md` presents reducer-owned machine sections for `What Improved`, `What Failed`, `Best Known State`, `Next Recommendation`, `Packaging Follow-Up`, `Mutation Coverage`, `Convergence Eligibility`, and `Trade-Off Detection`, but the visible reducer never opens or writes that file. `reduce-state.cjs` only emits `experiment-registry.json` and `agent-improvement-dashboard.md`, so most of the promised strategy-surface artifacts cannot survive the shipped reducer path. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md:71-137`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:489-503`)
- The dimensional-progress contract does survive. README says the reducer renders a Dimensional Progress table and stops when dimensions plateau, and the reducer both renders that table and adds an `all dimensions plateaued` stop reason when the plateau window condition is met. This is the clearest operator-visible contract that remains self-compliant in the visible path. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/README.md:163-169`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:305-325`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:371-391`)
- Trade-off annotations do not survive the visible dashboard path. `improvement_strategy.md` reserves a `Trade-Off Detection` table plus hard/soft resolution guidance, but `renderProfileSection()` only outputs repeated failure modes plus dimensional progress and `renderDashboard()` adds only summary, guardrails, stop status, and recommendation. No trade-off table or action annotation is rendered in the shipped dashboard output. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md:125-136`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:394-475`)
- Benchmark stability wording is lost between the published contract and the reducer surface. README says promotion depends on benchmark status and repeatability evidence, and SKILL says `benchmark-stability.cjs` emits a weight-recommendation report only after a minimum session-count threshold, but the dashboard only shows benchmark run/pass/fail counts, best benchmark score, and a generic benchmark-failure recommendation. There is no replay-count, stability-coefficient, repeatability, or `insufficient data` label in the visible reducer output. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/README.md:159-165`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:318-323`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:404-438`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:444-475`)
- Stop-reason and session-outcome wording drift before reaching the operator-facing dashboard. The changelog says sessions should report exact `STOP_REASONS` and `SESSION_OUTCOMES`, and SKILL publishes the outcome labels plus `blockedStop` gate-bundle language, but the reducer collapses stop status to booleans and free-text reasons while only counting `Keep-baseline results` in the global summary. The visible dashboard never renders enum-style stop reasons or session outcomes such as `promoted`, `rolledBack`, or `advisoryOnly`. (`.opencode/changelog/15--sk-improve-agent/v1.1.0.0.md:15-18`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:261-289`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:259-332`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:440-468`)

## Ruled Out
- This is not universal contract drift. README's `mirror drift as packaging work` guidance survives in the dashboard guardrails, and the dimensional-progress/plateau path is also visibly implemented. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/README.md:167-169`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:458-463`)

## Dead Ends
- I did not inspect `scripts/improvement-journal.cjs` or the `/improve:agent` YAML emitters in this pass, so I cannot yet tell whether exact stop reasons and session outcomes are produced upstream and then dropped by the reducer, or whether the visible workflow path never emits them at all.

## Sources Consulted
- `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/deep-research-strategy.md:36-53`
- `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:42-43`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/README.md:159-169`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:261-323`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md:71-137`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:259-503`
- `.opencode/changelog/15--sk-improve-agent/v1.1.0.0.md:15-18`

## Reflection
- What worked and why: Comparing the contract documents directly against the reducer write targets made it easy to separate promises that survive in the shipped dashboard from promises that stop at documentation.
- What did not work and why: The visible reducer path does not show whether richer stop/outcome fields exist upstream in the journal or YAML emitters, so the drift boundary is still scoped to the published reducer/dashboard surface.
- What I would do differently: I would inspect the journal emitter and `/improve:agent` auto workflow next so the next pass can locate the exact stage where enum-rich stop and outcome fields are lost.

## Recommended Next Focus
Stay on D4 for one more pass, but move upstream from the dashboard to `scripts/improvement-journal.cjs`, `.opencode/command/improve/agent.md`, and the visible improve-agent YAML workflow. The most productive next step is to determine whether exact stop reasons, session outcomes, and gate-result payloads are emitted correctly and then discarded by `reduce-state.cjs`, or whether the workflow never emits the richer contract in the first place. That would close the self-compliance story end-to-end before rotating back to D5 coverage-graph usage in the improvement loop.

 succeeded in 0ms:
# Iteration 20: D5 Improve-Agent Coverage Graph Visible Loop Audit

## Focus
This iteration rotated back to D5 for one final upstream-to-downstream audit of `sk-improve-agent` coverage-graph usage. The scope was limited to documented graph-aware claims versus the visible `/improve:agent` command, autonomous workflow, and reducer path to determine whether coverage data actually influences focus selection, stop gating, or contradiction-style blocking.

## Findings
- `SKILL.md` says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch and that the orchestrator skips mutation types already in the exhausted log, but the visible autonomous loop never invokes `mutation-coverage.cjs` before candidate generation. The shipped loop proceeds from integration scan directly to candidate generation, scoring, benchmarking, ledger append, reducer refresh, and reducer-driven stop check, so coverage state does not visibly steer mutation focus selection. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:137-158`)
- The runtime config enables a coverage-graph file and the strategy template reserves reducer-populated `Mutation Coverage` and `Convergence Eligibility` sections, but `reduce-state.cjs` never opens `mutation-coverage.json`. Its main path reads only `agent-improvement-state.jsonl`, config, and mirror-drift report before writing `experiment-registry.json` and `agent-improvement-dashboard.md`, so graph data does not enter the operator-visible dashboard or recommendation surfaces. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json:92-99`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md:114-123`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:489-503`)
- The helper itself does contain graph-aware logic: it records mutation attempts, exhaustion state, and per-iteration trajectories, and it computes convergence eligibility from recent dimension stability. But that logic remains encapsulated inside `mutation-coverage.cjs`; the visible command/workflow/reducer path inspected here exposes no step that consumes those helper outputs during the live loop. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:80-92`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:192-204`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:229-290`, `.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:272-286`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:137-158`)
- Visible stop gating is reducer-driven rather than graph-driven. `checkConvergenceEligibility()` would reject convergence for insufficient or unstable trajectory data, and the strategy template expects a verdict derived from trajectory plus exhaustion, but the actual stop path uses only trailing ties, infra failures, weak benchmark runs, drift ambiguity, and dimension plateau from the JSONL ledger. Coverage-graph convergence eligibility never feeds `step_stop_check`. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:229-272`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md:116-123`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:259-332`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:152-158`)
- I found no visible contradiction-oriented graph gate in the operator path. The published improve-agent workflow enumerates scan, propose, score, benchmark, append, reduce, and stop steps, and the reducer’s stop evaluator uses no contradiction input or graph query surface, so contradiction handling remains absent from the visible coverage-graph loop rather than participating in stop blocking. (`.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:272-286`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:137-158`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:259-325`)

## Ruled Out
- This is not because the graph was disabled in configuration. The shipped config explicitly enables `coverageGraph` and points it at `improvement/mutation-coverage.json`, so the gap is consumption in the visible loop, not a disabled feature flag. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json:92-95`)

## Dead Ends
- I did not locate a separate visible orchestrator runtime file beyond the published command/YAML surfaces that might call `mutation-coverage.cjs` programmatically, so the conclusion stays scoped to the shipped operator-facing path rather than every possible hidden executor.

## Sources Consulted
- `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/deep-research-strategy.md:48-52`
- `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:36-37`
- `.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:272-286`
- `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:137-167`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json:92-99`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_strategy.md:114-123`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:80-92`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:192-204`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:229-290`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:259-332`
- `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:489-503`

## Reflection
- What worked and why: Comparing the helper contract, runtime template expectations, and the visible YAML/reducer execution path made it easy to separate “graph exists” from “graph influences decisions.”
- What did not work and why: Negative evidence still depends on the published operator path, so I cannot prove that no hidden executor ever calls the helper elsewhere.
- What I would do differently: I would inspect `score-candidate.cjs` and any unseen orchestrator wrapper next if a follow-on phase needs to test whether graph-aware decisions exist off the documented path.

## Recommended Next Focus
If this research spins into a follow-on packet, the next productive move is not another broad loop audit but a narrow implementation-gap triage for `sk-improve-agent`: inspect `score-candidate.cjs`, any hidden orchestrator wrapper, and the confirm-mode workflow for off-path graph consumption, then decide whether to wire `mutation-coverage.cjs` into live focus/stop decisions or downgrade the docs/templates so they stop implying graph-driven behavior that the visible loop does not perform.
